classes.name: tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests

methods.name: tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests.setUp
documentation: Create a fount and drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests.test_tubeYieldsFiredDeferred
documentation:
When a tube yields a fired L{Deferred} its result is synchronously
delivered.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests.test_tubeYieldsUnfiredDeferred
documentation:
When a tube yields an unfired L{Deferred} its result is asynchronously
delivered.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests.test_tubeYieldsMultipleDeferreds
documentation:
When a tube yields multiple deferreds their results should be delivered
in order.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests.test_tubeYieldedDeferredFiresWhileFlowIsPaused
documentation:
When a L{Tube} yields an L{Deferred} and that L{Deferred} fires when
the L{_SiphonFount} is paused it should buffer it's result and deliver
it when L{_SiphonFount.resumeFlow} is called.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests.test_tubeStoppedDeferredly
tubes.test.test_undefer.DeferredIntegrationTests.test_fountToDeferred
documentation:
L{fountToDeferred} returns a L{Deferred} that fires with an iterable of
all the objects that the fount passed to it emits.
Tests for L{deferredToResult}.

classes.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_flowTo
documentation:
L{iteratorFount.flowTo} sets its drain and calls C{flowingFrom} on its
argument, returning that value.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_flowToDeliversValues
documentation:
L{iteratorFount.flowTo} will deliver all of its values to the given
drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_pauseFlow
documentation: L{iteratorFount.pauseFlow} will pause the delivery of items.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_unpauseFlow
documentation:
When all pauses returned by L{iteratorFount.pauseFlow} have been
unpaused, the flow resumes.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_stopFlow
documentation:
L{iteratorFount.stopFlow} stops the flow, propagating a C{flowStopped}
call to its drain and ceasing delivery immediately.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_stopIterationStopsIteration
documentation:
When the iterator passed to L{iteratorFount} is exhausted
L{IDrain.flowStopped} is called with L{StopIteration} as it's
reason.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_stopFlowCalledAfterFlowStopped
documentation:
L{iteratorFount} will only call its C{drain}'s L{flowStopped} once when
C{stopFlow} is called after the flow has stopped due to iterator
exhaustion.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_stopPausedFlow
documentation:
When L{iteratorFount} is stopped after being paused, the drain will
receive a C{flowStopped} when it is resumed.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_flowUnpausedAfterPausedFlowIsStopped
documentation:
When L{iteratorFount} is stopped after being paused, and subsequently
unpaused it should not start flowing again.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_memory.IteratorFountTests.test_provides
documentation: An L{iteratorFount} provides L{IFount}.
Tests for L{tubes.memory.iteratorFount}.

classes.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.setUp
documentation: Sert up these tests.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.adaptedProtocol
tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_flowToSetsDrain
documentation:
L{_TransportFount.flowTo} will set the C{drain} attribute of the
L{_TransportFount}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_flowToDeliversData
documentation:
L{_TransportFount.flowTo} will cause subsequent calls to
L{_ProtocolPlumbing.dataReceived} to invoke L{receive} on its drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_drainReceivingWritesToTransport
documentation:
Calling L{receive} on a L{_TransportDrain} will send the data to the
wrapped transport.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_stopFlowStopsConnection
documentation:
L{_TransportFount.stopFlow} will close the underlying connection by
calling C{loseConnection} on it.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_flowStoppedStopsConnection
documentation:
L{_TransportDrain.flowStopped} will close the underlying connection by
calling C{loseConnection} on it.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_connectionLostSendsFlowStopped
documentation:
When C{connectionLost} is called on a L{_ProtocolPlumbing} and it has
an L{IFount} flowing to it (in other words, flowing to its
L{_TransportDrain}), but no drain flowing I{from} it, the L{IFount}
should have C{stopFlow} invoked on it so that it will no longer deliver
to the now-dead transport.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_connectionLostSendsStopFlow
documentation:
L{_ProtocolPlumbing.connectionLost} will notify its C{_drain}'s
C{fount} that it should stop flowing, since the connection is now gone.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_dataReceivedBeforeFlowing
documentation:
If L{_ProtocolPlumbing.dataReceived} is called before its
L{_TransportFount} is flowing to anything, then it will pause the
transport but only until the L{_TransportFount} is flowing to
something.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_dataReceivedBeforeFlowingThenFlowTo
documentation:
Repeated calls to L{flowTo} don't replay the buffer from
L{dataReceived} to the new drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_dataReceivedWhenFlowingToNone
documentation:
Initially flowing to L{None} is the same as flowTo never having been
called, so L{_ProtocolPlumbing.dataReceived} should have the same
effect.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_flowingToNoneAfterFlowingToSomething
documentation:
Flowing to L{None} should disconnect from any drain, no longer
delivering it output.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_flowingFromAttribute
documentation:
L{_TransportDrain.flowingFrom} will establish the appropriate L{IFount}
to deliver L{pauseFlow} notifications to.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_pauseUnpauseFromTransport
documentation:
When an L{IFount} produces too much data for a L{_TransportDrain} to
process, the L{push producer
<twisted.internet.interfaces.IPushProducer>} associated with the
L{_TransportDrain}'s transport will relay the L{pauseProducing}
notification to that L{IFount}'s C{pauseFlow} method.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_pauseUnpauseFromOtherDrain
documentation:
When a L{_TransportFount} produces too much data for a L{drain
<IDrain>} to process, and it calls L{_TransportFount.pauseFlow}, the
underlying transport will be paused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_stopProducing
documentation:
When C{stopProducing} is called on the L{push producer
<twisted.internet.interfaces.IPushProducer>} associated with the
L{_TransportDrain}'s transport, the L{_TransportDrain}'s C{fount}'s
C{stopFlow} method will be invoked.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_flowingFrom
documentation:
L{_TransportFount.flowTo} returns the result of its argument's
C{flowingFrom}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowConnectorTests.test_flowingFromTwice
documentation:
L{_TransportDrain.flowingFrom} switches the producer registered with
the underlying transport.

Tests for L{flowFromEndpoint} and the drain/fount/factory adapters it
constructs.

classes.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_fromEndpoint
documentation:
L{flowFountFromEndpoint} returns a L{Deferred} that fires when the
listening port is ready.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_oneConnectionAccepted
documentation:
When a connection comes in to a listening L{flowFountFromEndpoint}, the
L{Listener} that it's flowing to's callback is called.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_acceptBeforeActuallyListening
documentation:
Sometimes a connection is established reentrantly by C{listen}, without
waiting for the L{Deferred} returned to fire.  In this case the
connection will be buffered until said L{Deferred} fires.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_acceptAfterDeferredButBeforeFlowTo
documentation:
If the L{Deferred} returned by L{flowFountFromEndpoint} fires, but the
callback doesn't immediately call C{flowTo} on the result, the
listening port will be paused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_readConnectionLost
documentation:
The protocol created by L{flowFountFromEndpoint} provides half-close
support, and when it receives an EOF (i.e.: C{readConnectionLost}) it
will signal the end of the flow to its fount's drain, but not to its
drain's fount.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_writeConnectionLost
documentation:
The protocol created by L{flowFountFromEndpoint} provides half-close
support, and when it receives an EOF (i.e.: C{writeConnectionLost}) it
will signal the end of the flow to its drain's fount, but not to its
fount's drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_backpressure
documentation:
When the L{IFount} returned by L{flowFountFromEndpoint} is paused, it
removes its listening port from the reactor.  When resumed, it re-adds
it.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_protocol.FlowListenerTests.test_stopping
documentation:
The L{IFount} returned by L{flowFountFromEndpoint} will stop listening
on the endpoint
Tests for L{flowFountFromEndpoint} and the fount adapter it constructs.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_kit.PauserTests.test_pauseOnce
documentation: One call to L{_Pauser.pause} will call the actuallyPause callable.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_kit.PauserTests.test_pauseThenUnpause
documentation:
A call to L{_Pauser.pause} followed by a call to the result's
C{unpause} will call the C{actuallyResume} callable.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_kit.PauserTests.test_secondUnpauseFails
documentation:
The second of two consectuive calls to L{IPause.unpause} results in an
L{AlreadyUnpaused} exception.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_kit.PauserTests.test_repeatedlyPause
documentation:
Multiple calls to L{_Pauser.pause} where not all of the pausers are
unpaused do not result in any calls to C{actuallyResume}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_kit.PauserTests.test_reentrantPause
documentation:
A L{Pauser} that pauses re-entrantly will only result in one call to
the specified C{pause} callable.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_kit.PauserTests.test_reentrantResume
documentation: A L{Pauser} that resumes re-entrantly will raise L{AlreadyUnpaused}.

Tests for L{Pauser}, helper for someone who wants to implement a thing
that pauses.

The C{outputType} argument to L{Router}'s constructor specifies the

methods.name: tubes.test.test_routing.RoutedTests.test_ne

methods.name: tubes.test.test_routing.RoutedTests.test_providedBy

L{Routed.providedBy} ensures that the given object is L{to} but makes
no assertions about its payload if given L{Routed} is given no
sub-specification.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FakeIntermediateDrain.flowingFrom

documentation:
An intermediate drain that returns a subsequent fount, for debugging
tube-like transceivers with more explicit ordering.

classes.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_outFountFlowTo
documentation:
L{Out.newFount}'s C{flowTo} calls C{flowingFrom} on its drain and
returns the result.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_verifyCompliance
documentation:
L{Out.newFount} and L{Out.drain} adhere to their respected declared
interfaces.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_fanOut
documentation:
When an L{Out} is constructed and flowed to two drains, both drains
receive the same value passed to L{Out.drain}'s C{receive} method.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_fanReceivesBeforeFountsHaveDrains
documentation:
L{Out.drain}'s C{receive} method only relays outputs to founts which
are presently attached.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_pausingOneOutFountPausesUpstreamFount
documentation:
When one fount created by L{Out.newFount} is paused, the fount flowing
to L{Out.drain} is paused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_oneFountPausesInReceive
documentation:
When an L{Out} has two founts created by C{newFount} fA and fB, and
they are flowing to two drains, dA and dB, if dA pauses its fount
during C{receive(X)}, C{X} will still be delivered to dB, because dB
hasn't paused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_oneFountPausesOthersInReceive
documentation:
When an L{Out} has two founts created by C{newFount} fA and fB, and
they are flowing to two drains, dA and dB, if dA pauses both founts
during C{receive(X)}, C{X} will I{not} be delivered to dB, because fB
is paused by the time that call would be made.  Unpausing both will
cause it to get delivered.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_oneFountStops
documentation:
When one fount created by L{Out.newFount} is stopped, only the drain
for that fount is affected; others continue receiving values.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_oneFountStopsInReceive
documentation:
When one fount created by L{Out.newFount} is stopped in its drain's
C{receive} method, only the drain for that fount is affected; others
continue receiving values.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_switchFlowToNone
documentation: When L{out.drain} removes its upstream fount, it unpauses it.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanOutTests.test_flowStopped
documentation:
When the flow stops to L{out.drain}, it stops to all downstream drains
as well, with the same reason.
Tests for L{tubes.fan.Out}

classes.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests.test_oneDrainReceives
documentation:
When one drain created by L{In.newDrain} recives a value, the drain
that L{In.fount} is flowing to receives that value.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests.test_pauseWhenNoDrain
documentation:
When a drain created by L{In.newDrain} is hooked up to a new fount, but
that L{In.fount} isn't flowing to anything yet, the new fount will be
paused immediately; when the L{In.fount} receives a drain, it is
unpaused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests.test_pauseNewFountWhenPaused
documentation:
When a drain created by L{In.newDrain} receives a new fount, if
L{In.fount} is already paused, the fount flowed to the new drain will
also be paused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests.test_dontUnpauseWhenNoDrain
documentation: L{In.fount}C{.flowTo(None)} won't unpause L{In}'s upstream founts.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests.test_pauseWhenSwitchedToNoDrain
documentation:
L{In.fount}C{.flowTo(None)} after L{In.fount} already has a drain will
pause all the upstream founts.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests.test_flowStopped
documentation:
When the flow stops to one of the drains returned by L{In.newDrain}, it
removes the associated fount from the list of founts to be paused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanInTests.test_stopFlow
documentation:
When the drain of L{In.fount} stops its upstream flow, that stops the
flow of every attached fount.
Tests for L{tubes.fan.In}.

classes.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanThruTests

methods.name: tubes.test.test_fan.FanThruTests.test_thru
documentation:
Each input provided to L{Thru} will be sent to each of its drains, and
the outputs of those then sent on to its downstream drain in order.
Tests for L{Thru}.

classes.name: tubes.itube.IFount

methods.name: tubes.itube.IFount.flowTo
documentation:
Add a drain to this fount to consume its output.

This will I{synchronously} call L{flowingFrom(fount)
<IDrain.flowingFrom>} on C{drain} to indicate to C{drain} which
L{IFount} its future input will come from - I{unless} this L{IFount} is
exhausted and will never produce more output.  In this case, C{flowTo}
must I{not} call C{flowingFrom}, and must return L{None}.

Typically, this will return the result of L{drain.flowingFrom(fount)
<IDrain.flowingFrom>} to allow construction of pipelines with the
C{x.flowTo(...).flowTo(...).flowTo(...)} idiom; however,
implementations of L{IFount} are at liberty to return L{None} or any
valid L{IFount}.

@raise AlreadyDraining: if there is already a drain (i.e.  C{flowTo}
    has already been called on this L{IFount}.)

@return: another L{IFount} provider, or C{None}.  By convention, this
    will return the value of C{flowingFrom} and allow the drain to
    transform the C{outputType} (however, other transformations are
    allowed).

documentation:
A tube transforms input into output.

Look at this awesome ASCII art:

```
                 a fount
              +----+--------+               +-----  a
             / +---+------+ | | data flow  / +----  fount
 a tube --->/ /           | | v           / /
           / /            | |            / /
          / /             | |  a drain  / /
a     ---+ /              | +----+-----+ /<--- a tube
drain ----+               +------+------+

      =========> direction of flow =========>
```

(Image credit Nam Nguyen)

@note: L\{ITube} providers participate in I{data processing} not in I{flow

: control}. That is to say, an L\{ITube} provider can translate its input
  to output, but cannot impact the rate at which that output is
  delivered. If you want to implement flow-control modifications,
  implement L\{IDrain} directly. L\{IDrain} providers may be easily
  connected up to L\{ITube} providers with L{series
  \<tubes.tube.series>}, so you may implement flow-control in an
  L\{IDrain} that passes on its input unmodified and data-processing in an
  L\{ITube} and hook them together.

modules.name: tubes.routing
documentation:
A L\{Router} receives items with addressing information and dispatches them to
an appropriate output, stripping the addressing information off.

Use like so:

```
from tubes.tube import receiver, series
from tubes.routing import Router, to

aRouter = Router()
evens = aRouter.newRoute()
odds = aRouter.newRoute()

@receiver()
def evenOdd(item):
    if (item % 2) == 0:
        yield to(evens, item)
    else:
        yield to(odds, item)

numbers.flowTo(series(evenOdd, aRouter.drain))
```

Assuming C\{numbers} is a fount of counting integers, this creates two founts:
C\{evens} and C\{odds}, whose outputs are even and odd integers, respectively.
Note that C\{evenOdd} also uses C\{evens} and C\{odds} as I\{addresses}; the first
argument to L\{to} says I\{where} the value will go.

Why do this rather than just having C\{evenOdd} just call methods directly based
on whether a number is even or odd?

By using a L\{Router}, flow control relationships are automatically preserved by
the same mechanism that tubes usually use. The distinct drains of C\{evens} and
C\{odds} can both independently pause their founts, and the pause state will be
propagated to the "numbers" fount. If you want to send on outputs to multiple
drains which may have complex flow-control interrelationships, you can't do
that by calling the C\{receive} method directly since any one of those methods
might reentrantly pause its fount.
-*- test-case-name: tubes.test.test_routing -*-
Copyright (c) Twisted Matrix Laboratories.
See LICENSE for details.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.setUp
documentation: Create a siphon, and a fake drain and fount connected to it.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeStarted
documentation: The L{_Siphon} starts its L{Tube} upon C{flowingFrom}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_startedFlowingToAnother
documentation:
The greeting is relayed to the ultimate drain when a tube in the middle
of a series adds a greeting via C{started}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_noDrainThenLoseFount
documentation:
If a fount is flowed to a tube which does not yet have a drain, then
flowed to another place, it will not be paused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonFlowingFromReturnsSelfFount
documentation:
L{_SiphonDrain.flowingFrom} initially returns its L{_Siphon}'s
downstream fount.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonFlowingFromNoneReturnsSelfFount
documentation:
L{_SiphonDrain.flowingFrom} initially returns its L{_Siphon}'s
downstream fount when passed L{None} as well.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonFlowingFromSomethingThenNothing
documentation:
L{_SiphonDrain.flowingFrom} sets L{_SiphonDrain.fount}, whether it is
passed a valid L{IFount} (one with matching input/output types) or
L{None}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonFlowingFromReturnsNextFount
documentation:
Once L{_SiphonFount.flowTo} has been called,
L{_SiphonDrain.flowingFrom} returns the next fount in the chain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeReStarted
documentation:
It's perfectly valid to take a L{_Siphon} and call C{flowingFrom} with
the same drain it's already flowing to.

This will happen any time that a series is partially constructed and
then flowed to a new drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeStopped
documentation:
The L{_Siphon} stops its L{Tube} and propagates C{flowStopped}
downstream upon C{flowStopped}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeDiverting
documentation:
The L{_Siphon} of a L{Tube} sends on data to a newly specified
L{IDrain} when its L{IDivertable.divert} method is called.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeDivertingReassembly
documentation:
The L{_Siphon} of a L{Tube} sends on reassembled data - the return
value of L{Tube.reassemble} to a newly specified L{Drain}; it is only
called with un-consumed elements of data (those which have never been
passed to C{receive}).

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_diverterInYourDiverterSoYouCanDivertWhileYouDivert
documentation:
When L{IDivertable.reassemble} returns multiple values, the argument to
L{Diverter.divert}, B, may itself call L{Diverter.divert} with a drain
C to redirect the flow as it's receiving those values and subsequent
values will be delivered to C{C.receive}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_divertWhilePaused
documentation:
If an L{IDivertable} L{tube} is diverted while it is paused,
L{reassemble} will still be passed the rest of the values.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeDivertingControlsWhereOutputGoes
documentation:
If a siphon A with a tube Ap is flowing to a siphon B with a divertable
tube Bp, Ap.received may switch B to a drain C, and C will receive any
outputs produced by that received call; B (and Bp) will not.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubePausesItself
documentation:
When one of the methods on L{Tube} pauses its own C{fount} or C{drain},
the next item it yields will not arrive at its downstream drain until
it is unpaused.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_initiallyEnthusiasticFountBecomesDisillusioned
documentation:
If an L{IFount} provider synchronously calls C{receive} on a
L{_SiphonDrain}, whose corresponding L{_SiphonFount} is not flowing to
an L{IDrain} yet, it will be synchronously paused with
L{IFount.pauseFlow}; when that L{_SiphonFount} then flows to something
else, the buffer will be unspooled.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_flowToNoneInitialNoOp
documentation:
L{_SiphonFount.flowTo}C{(None)} is a no-op when called before
any other invocations of L{_SiphonFount.flowTo}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeDiverting_ReEntrantResumeReceive
documentation:
Diverting a tube that is receiving data from a fount which
synchronously produces some data to C{receive} will ... uh .. work.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_tubeDiverting_LotsOfStuffAtOnce
documentation:
If a tube returns a sequence of multiple things, great.

(This is a test for diverting when a receive method has returned
multiple things.)

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_flowingFromFirst
documentation:
If L{_Siphon.flowingFrom} is called before L{_Siphon.flowTo}, the
argument to L{_Siphon.flowTo} will immediately have its
L{IDrain.flowingFrom} called.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonReceiveCallsTubeReceived
documentation:
L{_SiphonDrain.receive} will call C{tube.received} and synthesize a
fake "0.5" progress result if L{None} is returned.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_flowFromTypeCheckFails
documentation:
L{_Siphon.flowingFrom} checks the type of its input.  If it doesn't
match (both are specified explicitly, and they don't match).

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_flowFromTypeCheckSucceeds
documentation:
L{_Siphon.flowingFrom} checks the type of its input.  If it doesn't
match (both are specified explicitly, and they don't match).

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_receiveIterableDeliversDownstream
documentation:
When L{Tube.received} yields a value, L{_Siphon} will call L{receive}
on its downstream drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_receiveCallsTubeReceived
documentation:
L{_SiphonDrain.receive} will send its input to L{ITube.received} on its
tube.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_flowToWillNotResumeFlowPausedInFlowingFrom
documentation:
L{_SiphonFount.flowTo} will not call L{_SiphonFount.resumeFlow} when
it's L{IDrain} calls L{IFount.pauseFlow} in L{IDrain.flowingFrom}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_reentrantFlowTo
documentation:
An L{IDrain} may call its argument's L{_SiphonFount.flowTo} method in
L{IDrain.flowingFrom} and said fount will be flowing to the new drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_drainPausesFlowWhenPreviouslyPaused
documentation:
L{_SiphonDrain.flowingFrom} will pause its fount if its L{_SiphonFount}
was previously paused, and unpause its old fount.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_drainFlowingFromNoneAlsoUnpauses
documentation:
L{_SiphonDrain.flowingFrom} will resume its old fount when flowed to
L{None}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_drainRemainsPausedAcrossDetachedState
tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonDrainRepr
documentation: repr for L{_SiphonDrain} includes a reference to its tube.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonFountRepr
documentation: repr for L{_SiphonFount} includes a reference to its tube.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_siphonRepr
documentation: repr for L{_Siphon} includes a reference to its tube.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_diverterRepr
documentation: repr for L{Diverter} includes a reference to its tube.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_stopFlow
documentation:
L{_SiphonFount.stopFlow} stops the flow of its L{_Siphon}'s upstream
fount.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_stopFlowInterruptsStarted
documentation:
As per L{IFount.stopFlow}, a compliant L{fount <IFount>} never calls
C{received} on its C{drain} after receiving a C{stopFlow} request; so,
when a L{tube} yields multiple values from C{started}, only those
delivered before C{stopFlow} is called should be delivered.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_stopFlowStopsFlowImmediately
documentation:
Similar to L{test_stopFlowInterruptsStarted}, if the upstream fount
calls C{flowStopped} within its C{stopFlow} implementation.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_stopFlowBeforeFlowBegins
documentation:
L{_SiphonFount.stopFlow} will stop the flow of its L{_Siphon}'s
upstream fount later, when it acquires one, if it's previously been
stopped.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_stopFlowWhileStartingFlow
documentation:
If a fount flowing to a tube calls C{flowStopped} in C{flowTo}, the
results of C{started} and C{stopped} on the tube should both show up to
its drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_seriesStartsWithSeries
documentation:
If L{series} is called with the result of L{series} as its first
argument, then L{series}' second argument will receive values from the
last of the arguments to the first call to L{series}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.SeriesTests.test_seriesEndsInTerminalDrain
documentation:
If L{series} is called with an L{IDrain} which returns L{None} from
C{flowingFrom}, then the return value from L{series} will return
L{None} from its L{flowingFrom}.
Tests for L{series}.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.ErrorBehaviorTests.test_startedRaises

If L{ITube.started} raises an exception, the exception will be logged,
the tube's fount will have L{IFount.stopFlow} called, and
L{IDrain.flowStopped} will be called on the tube's downstream drain.

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.ErrorBehaviorTests.test_startedRaisesNoDrain
documentation:
If L{ITube.started} raises an exception, the exception will be logged,
the tube's fount will have L{IFount.stopFlow} called, and
L{IDrain.flowStopped} will be called on the tube's downstream drain.
Test cases for when unexpected exceptions are raised.

If L{next} on the iterator returned from L{ITube.started} (OR OTHER)
raises an exception, the exception will be logged, and...

If L{next} on the iterator returned from L{ITube.started} (OR OTHER)

methods.name: tubes.test.test_tube.TodoTests.test_reassembleRaises

methods.name: tubes.fan.Out.__init__
documentation: Create an L{Out}.

methods.name: tubes.fan.Out.newFount

documentation:
A fan.L\{Out} presents a single L{drain \<IDrain>} that delivers the inputs
to multiple L{founts \<IFount>}:

```
                                   /--> Out.newFount() --> your drain
                                  /
your fount --> Out.drain --> Out <----> Out.newFount() --> your drain
                                  \
                                   \--> Out.newFount() --> your drain
```

@ivar drain: The fount which produces all new attributes.
@type drain: L\{IDrain}

classes.name: tubes.fan.Thru

methods.name: tubes.fan.Thru.__init__
tubes.fan.Thru.flowingFrom

documentation:
A fan.L\{Thru} takes an input and fans it I\{thru} multiple
drains-which-produce-founts, such as L{tubes \<tubes.itube.ITube>}:

```
        Your Fount
     (producing "foo")
            |
            v
          Thru
            |
          _/|\_
        _/  |  \_
       /    |    \
foo2bar  foo2baz  foo2qux
       \_   |   _/
         \_ | _/
           \|/
            |
            v
          Thru
            |
            v
        Your Drain
 (receiving a combination
     of foo, bar, baz)
```

The way you would construct such a flow in code would be:

```
yourFount.flowTo(Thru([series(foo2bar()),
                       series(foo2baz()),
                       series(foo2qux())])).flowTo(yourDrain)
```
